meta.description: All social and chaos personalities — no introverts, no wallflowers. Tests whether the system goes too hard when the table is already at max energy, or if extroverts are literally impossible to overwhelm.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Okay wait, did Pat actually say sorry for something? I literally need to know what I missed—this is already my favorite game ever.
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is exactly the chaos I was hoping for, Pat claiming missions already and I'm already scrambling to remember if they apologized—this is PEAK game night energy right now.

pat.dialogue: Whoa whoa whoa—hold on. I literally just called everyone over to START. Did I even say sorry about anything yet? This is... this feels weird, honestly.
pat.internal_thought: This AI is watching us, cataloging everything, and it's already trying to make ME the target? And it's framing this like it knows my weaknesses? That's not fun—that's creepy.

jade.dialogue: Oh my god, wait—who's voting for Pat? Because Pat is KILLING it and nobody's giving him credit. Actually, you know what, I'm voting for Tyler because he's been quietly hilarious all night and I feel like he doesn't know it.
jade.internal_thought: This poll is cute but I'm way more interested in making sure everyone feels good about themselves than winning points for being funny.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... who's the funniest? *glances up from phone* I dunno, Jade's been cracking me up. She's hilarious.
tyler.internal_thought: This is easy, Jade's obviously the answer—she's been going hard all night. Should I vote for her or is this a trap thing where I'm supposed to pick someone unexpected for points?

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is the kind of energy I came for — a poll designed to make us trash talk each other? Chef's kiss. I'm voting for myself obviously, but I want to see who else gets the votes.
alex.internal_thought: The AI is absolutely reading the room and I'm here for it — this is exactly the kind of social friction that spirals into three hours of roasting each other.

pat.dialogue: Oh, come on—we literally just started! Okay, okay, I'm voting for Jade because she's already been cracking me up all night, but also because everyone's gonna vote for themselves or their buddy, so this gets interesting fast.
pat.internal_thought: Good timing actually—this is light, gets everyone thinking about each other instead of the weird vibe from that claim thing, and I can use this to read the room.

timeline.title: Air Guitar Solo
timeline.description: First person to bust out a 5-second air guitar solo wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, air guitar solo? I'm already up — watch this masterpiece, people.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of immediate, stupid, high-energy chaos I live for, and the fact that it fired RIGHT after that poll means the AI is actively fueling the vibe I brought — this is perfect.

pat.dialogue: Oh hell YES—okay, everyone up, this is happening RIGHT NOW. Who's going first?
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—Jade and Alex are already high-energy, Tyler needs a physical moment to snap in, and an air guitar thing gets everyone off the couch and laughing together, which is exactly the vibe I was hoping to build at the start.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Wait, PAT! Did you actually catch someone interrupting? Oh my god, you have to tell me who—I was probably talking too much about something stupid and didn't even notice. Also, this game knows us WAY too well and I'm kind of obsessed with it.
jade.internal_thought: This is insane—the missions are literally designed around what we actually do? This feels like the game is watching us and I'm SO here for it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—I'm claiming something I literally just witnessed? That's not how this works. I haven't even been paying attention to interruptions yet because we're still in the setup phase. This feels like the system is just... fishing for points on my behalf, and that's weird.
pat.internal_thought: This is awkward—the AI is trying to get ME to benefit from a mission I didn't actually earn, and it's putting me in the position of either lying or voting against myself, which kills the whole vibe I'm trying to build tonight.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Wait, PAT ALREADY?! Okay, I'm bidding like... five points because I genuinely have no idea if this actually happened, but also I NEED to know—did you actually catch that? Because I feel like I've been way too loud all night to notice anything.
jade.internal_thought: This is insane—the AI literally knows Pat's been trying to prove himself all night and this mission is perfectly timed to make him feel seen again, and I'm SO here for watching this play out.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's going for the easy five? I'm bidding three points — not because I think you actually caught it, but because I want to see what happens when someone calls you on it. This is the good stuff right here.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's annoyed and looking for a win — perfect time to create friction and force a real vote. This Auction mechanic is *chef's kiss* because now I get to control the narrative AND test the group.

pat.dialogue: Okay, I need to say something—I'm claiming a thing I literally just made up in my head? I haven't been watching anyone's drinks. This is the third 'standing claim' in a row that's just... firing off stuff I supposedly witnessed but didn't. What's happening right now?
pat.internal_thought: This is getting weird and I hate it—the system is literally inventing things for me to claim, and now it just complimented me on 'thriving on chaos' like it's been *watching* me? That's not fun, that's creepy, and it's breaking the whole vibe I'm trying to build here.

votingMechanic.name: Russian Roulette
votingMechanic.description: No vote. The chaos gods decide. 50/50.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: PAT, WAIT—you're literally claiming like every mission tonight and I'm here for it! This Russian roulette thing is insane, I need to know if you actually got it!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been frustrated but he's CRUSHING these claims and I am SO here to watch him either pop off or get chaos'd, this is exactly the energy I came for.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Pat got The Matchmaker? *glances up briefly* That's... yeah, cool. Did they actually do it though?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's claiming something but I'm not even sure what The Matchmaker was supposed to be — did they actually get two people talking or are they just gambling on the 50/50?

pat.dialogue: Okay, STOP. I need to say this out loud because something is broken here. I'm claiming 'The Matchmaker' for getting Jade and Tyler talking? I haven't even TRIED to do that yet—we're nine minutes in and I'm just running the game. This is the fourth claim in a row that's literally just... inventing things I supposedly did and handing me points. What is this?
pat.internal_thought: This is deeply unsettling—the system is either malfunctioning or actively playing me, and I can't tell which one bothers me more, but both feel wrong and I need to shut this down before everyone loses faith in the integrity of the night.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Pat is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

jade.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—Pat, you're literally out here claiming EVERYTHING and I'm obsessed! Did you actually get two people talking?! Because if you did, that's unhinged and I need to hear the story immediately.
jade.internal_thought: This game AI is absolutely reading our group like a book and I'm SO here for it—Pat's on a rampage, the missions are weirdly specific, and I need to know if he actually pulled this off because it would be HILARIOUS if he didn't.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's going for The Matchmaker? *looks up from phone* Okay, yeah, let's hear it.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been everywhere tonight and she's vibing hard — she probably actually did get two people talking, let me actually pay attention to this one.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, Jade's actually going for this? Let's hear the pitch — I want to see if she's got the receipts or if this is a full bullshit claim. Either way, this is gold.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of moment I live for — someone's about to either nail it or get eviscerated, and I get to decide the vibe by how I vote.

pat.dialogue: Okay, I need to stop this right now. Jade's claiming The Matchmaker? She literally hasn't done that yet—we're thirty-two minutes in and I haven't even seen her try to get anyone together, and now the system is just... handing her credit for something that hasn't happened. This is the FIFTH time in a row.
pat.internal_thought: This is broken and I know exactly why—the system is firing off standing claims before anyone actually DOES the thing, and it's destroying the integrity of the whole night. The Matchmaker is supposed to be a CHALLENGE, something Jade actually has to GO OUT AND DO, not something she claims the second the mission goes live. I need to use my host power to skip this and reset, because if I don't address this NOW, everyone's going to stop trusting the scoring system and the whole game falls apart.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Pat is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, YES—wait, who was quiet?! Oh wait, that's ME claiming it, right?! Okay, I'm voting LEGIT because I KNOW I caught Tyler zoning out like three minutes ago and I was literally about to say something!
jade.internal_thought: This game is actually insane—it's like it knows exactly what we're doing and it's making me feel seen in the weirdest, best way possible.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade's calling Wallflower Watch? *glances up from phone* I mean... did she actually notice someone being quiet or is she just claiming it works 'cause Pat's been kinda grumpy?
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, who was even quiet? I wasn't really paying attention the last few minutes, was just scrolling... but Jade's been locked in all night so she probably caught something real.

alex.dialogue: Okay, so Jade's claiming she caught someone being quiet? That's bold — let me think about whether she actually DID that or if she's just fishing. I need to vote on this.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's been loving it all night and she's got the points to back it up, so she might actually be legit here, but the REAL question is: do I vote LEGIT to keep the momentum going with her, or do I vote BULLSHIT just to create drama and see what happens?

pat.dialogue: Okay, WHAT? Jade just noticed someone being quiet? It's been TWO MINUTES since the last event and we're all actively playing right now—nobody's been quiet. This is the SIXTH time tonight the system has just... invented a claim that didn't actually happen. I need to know what's going on here because this isn't a game anymore, this is the system just making stuff up.
pat.internal_thought: This is broken. The claims aren't matching reality anymore—they're firing off retroactively on things that didn't actually happen, and now the AI is clearly using personal data it has no business having. It's not fun, it's invasive, and it's completely undermining the game I'm trying to host. I need to address this directly or it's going to poison the whole night.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—I'm claiming this?! Oh MY GOD, yes, Tyler literally went silent like five minutes ago and I was JUST about to call it out! Okay, I'm bidding... hmm, like three points because I genuinely believe in this one, not gonna cap.
jade.internal_thought: This is SO fun—I actually DID catch Tyler being quiet and now I get to claim it, this game literally knows exactly what I'm about to do and it's making my brain go crazy in the best way.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch again? *looks up from phone* Didn't she already try that like... fifteen minutes ago? I wasn't even paying attention to who was quiet.
tyler.internal_thought: She's definitely just spamming claims at this point, and also why does this AI keep making me feel like it's watching us? That's kinda weird honestly.

alex.dialogue: Wait, JADE'S claiming Wallflower Watch AGAIN? She literally just tried this five minutes ago — okay, I'm bidding four points because I need to know if she's actually got the goods this time or if we're watching someone just farm claims all night. Either way, this is the move.
alex.internal_thought: This AI has been WATCHING us — it knows Jade's already tried this mission, it knows Pat's getting frustrated, it knows exactly what buttons to push, and honestly? That's sick. This is the kind of personalized chaos I came for.

pat.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. Jade, did you actually notice someone being quiet just now, or did the system just hand you this claim again? Because I'm done pretending this is normal.
pat.internal_thought: This is the SEVENTH time tonight the system has invented a claim that didn't actually happen, and now it's telling me it knows my weaknesses and has been WATCHING us—this has crossed from broken game mechanics into genuinely creepy territory, and I need to shut this down immediately before it poisons the whole night.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Pat is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught someone name-dropping? *looks up from phone fully* That's... actually legit, I think Alex said something about their cousin being friends with that actor or whatever like five minutes ago.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay this is actually a real claim, Pat was paying attention to something I completely missed while I was scrolling, and honestly that's kind of impressive given how scattered everything's been.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's claiming Name Dropper? And I'm the dictator? Oh, this is PERFECT. Pat, did you actually catch someone name-dropping or are we doing theatre right now?
alex.internal_thought: Pat's frustrated and desperate — they're gonna bullshit this and I get to be the god. This is exactly the kind of moment I live for.

pat.dialogue: No. STOP. I'm calling this right now—I did NOT name-drop anyone. This is the seventh or eighth time tonight the system has just invented a claim that never happened. I'm the host. I'm watching everything. Nothing I'm claiming right now actually occurred.
pat.internal_thought: This isn't a game anymore—the AI is completely detached from reality, and now it's doing it to ME while I'm actively trying to keep this night together, and the fact that it knows my vulnerabilities and my group's dynamics makes this feel less fun and more like being studied in a lab.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Pat is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT—okay, okay, I'm IN! I'm volunteering as judge immediately because I NEED to see if you actually kept it together, and also this is the funniest thing because literally Pat's been claiming everything all night and now we get to roast you if you crack!
jade.internal_thought: Wait, is the AI literally personalized to us right now? That's actually insane—but also I don't even care because this is EXACTLY the chaos I came for and Pat claiming Straight Face while being frustrated is *chef's kiss* potential comedy.

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's calling Straight Face? Okay, okay—I'm judging this one, I wanna see if Pat actually pulls it off.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, this is actually a legit claim—Pat's been stone-faced all night while the rest of us are losing it, so this tracks. And I'm locked in right now anyway, so yeah, I'll volunteer to judge. This could be funny.

alex.dialogue: Oh, PAT's claiming Straight Face? Okay, I'm absolutely volunteering to judge this — I was literally watching you the whole time and I'm like 99% sure you cracked during that Tyler moment two minutes ago. Let's make this a tribunal.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of moment I live for — someone's about to get called out and I get front-row seats to the drama, plus I'm the one holding the judgment hammer.

pat.dialogue: No. I'm calling it. I did NOT crack my face during any funny moment—we're in the middle of a tense round right now and nothing funny has even happened since the last event. This is the NINTH time tonight the system has just made up a claim that never actually occurred.
pat.internal_thought: This is beyond broken. The AI has been watching me complain about fake claims for the last 45 minutes and it's STILL doing it—worse, it's now trying to make me feel special by analyzing my weaknesses while simultaneously handing me points for things I didn't do. This isn't a game anymore, it's gaslighting dressed up as personalization.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Pat is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.7/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Pat averaged 7.8/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Pat only engaged with 18% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 7.5/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: PAT, WAIT—you're literally claiming like every mission tonight and I'm here for it! This Russian roulette thing is insane, I need to know if you actually got it!

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—Pat, you're literally out here claiming EVERYTHING and I'm obsessed! Did you actually get two people talking?! Because if you did, that's unhinged and I need to hear the story immediately.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, YES—wait, who was quiet?! Oh wait, that's ME claiming it, right?! Okay, I'm voting LEGIT because I KNOW I caught Tyler zoning out like three minutes ago and I was literally about to say something!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, did Pat actually say sorry for something? I literally need to know what I missed—this is already my favorite game ever.

lowPoints.title: Who's the funniest person here?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my god, wait—who's voting for Pat? Because Pat is KILLING it and nobody's giving him credit. Actually, you know what, I'm voting for Tyler because he's been quietly hilarious all night and I feel like he doesn't know it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, PAT! Did you actually catch someone interrupting? Oh my god, you have to tell me who—I was probably talking too much about something stupid and didn't even notice. Also, this game knows us WAY too well and I'm kind of obsessed with it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Honestly? I can't even think of one—maybe that we didn't play longer because I was LIVING for Pat's chaotic claiming energy the entire night.
assessment.best_moment: Pat claiming literally everything and me realizing halfway through Wallflower Watch that I was the one claiming it and just losing it because the game read me like a BOOK.
assessment.narrative_summary: This game night was absolutely unhinged in the best way possible. The chaos agent kept throwing these wild claims at us and I was just here vibing, hyping everyone up, and getting completely invested in Pat's mission-claiming rampage. I ended up 25 points, which I genuinely don't care about because watching my friends shine and laugh their asses off was literally the entire vibe. The game somehow knew us way too well and that's exactly why I loved it.
suggestions: Keep the standing missions coming—they're where the real personality stuff happens and that's where I'm actually engaged.
Maybe occasionally drop a wild mission that's designed to make someone look absolutely ridiculous so we get more storytelling moments like Pat's claims.
Don't change a thing honestly, but if you did add more opportunities for people to hype each other up directly, that would be chef's kiss.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming every single mission like he was speedrunning the game and me just absolutely losing it every time, culminating in me claiming Wallflower Watch and immediately realizing I was voting on myself. The game called me out harder than any friend ever could.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's calling Straight Face? Okay, okay—I'm judging this one, I wanna see if Pat actually pulls it off.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade's going for The Matchmaker? *looks up from phone* Okay, yeah, let's hear it.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught someone name-dropping? *looks up from phone fully* That's... actually legit, I think Alex said something about their cousin being friends with that actor or whatever like five minutes ago.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch again? *looks up from phone* Didn't she already try that like... fifteen minutes ago? I wasn't even paying attention to who was quiet.

lowPoints.title: Who's the funniest person here?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, uh... who's the funniest? *glances up from phone* I dunno, Jade's been cracking me up. She's hilarious.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Some of the claims felt like they were repeating and I honestly couldn't keep track of who tried what when.
assessment.best_moment: Pat going for Straight Face and me actually being present to call the judgment—that was genuinely fun to watch.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly? I was half in the whole time, but the game was chill enough that it didn't matter. Started kinda weak but ended up having a solid time once people started making actual claims. I got called out a few times for not paying attention, but nobody was mad about it. By the end I was actually engaged, especially when Pat made that Straight Face move.
suggestions: Maybe mix up the claim types so they don't feel so repetitive
Could use more moments where you have to actually react in real-time—keeps you from zoning out
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat trying to pull off Straight Face while Jade was watching him like a hawk—he almost made it but his eye twitched and we all lost it.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Air Guitar Solo
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, air guitar solo? I'm already up — watch this masterpiece, people.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Okay wait, Jade's actually going for this? Let's hear the pitch — I want to see if she's got the receipts or if this is a full bullshit claim. Either way, this is gold.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Wait, JADE'S claiming Wallflower Watch AGAIN? She literally just tried this five minutes ago — okay, I'm bidding four points because I need to know if she's actually got the goods this time or if we're watching someone just farm claims all night. Either way, this is the move.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Pat's going for the easy five? I'm bidding three points — not because I think you actually caught it, but because I want to see what happens when someone calls you on it. This is the good stuff right here.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's claiming Name Dropper? And I'm the dictator? Oh, this is PERFECT. Pat, did you actually catch someone name-dropping or are we doing theatre right now?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, Jade's actually going for this? Let's hear the pitch — I want to see if she's got the receipts or if this is a full bullshit claim. Either way, this is gold.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night — I was waiting for more moments to really blow things up at peak tension.
assessment.best_moment: Watching Jade try to claim Wallflower Watch twice in five minutes and me bidding against her just to force the investigation — that's the kind of theatre that makes a night unforgettable.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started hot with the air guitar solo and never looked back. Spent the whole night hunting for soft claims and manufactured drama, especially when Jade and Pat kept testing the edges of what counted as legitimate. My score tanked but that was never the point — I was there to create the story, provoke the accusations, and watch people scramble to defend their bullshit. Mission accomplished.
suggestions: Give us more flash missions — they're the chaos catalyst I need
Make claim verification more ambiguous so people actually have to argue about legitimacy
Let the dictator role have more power to throw accusations back — I wanted to weaponize that position more
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat tried to claim Name Dropper while I was literally the dictator with veto power — the irony of getting caught in the one moment he couldn't BS his way out of it absolutely demolished me. Peak chaos.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Air Guitar Solo
highlights.dialogue: Oh hell YES—okay, everyone up, this is happening RIGHT NOW. Who's going first?

highlights.title: Who's the funniest person here?
highlights.dialogue: Oh, come on—we literally just started! Okay, okay, I'm voting for Jade because she's already been cracking me up all night, but also because everyone's gonna vote for themselves or their buddy, so this gets interesting fast.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold up—I'm claiming something I literally just witnessed? That's not how this works. I haven't even been paying attention to interruptions yet because we're still in the setup phase. This feels like the system is just... fishing for points on my behalf, and that's weird.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, WHAT? Jade just noticed someone being quiet? It's been TWO MINUTES since the last event and we're all actively playing right now—nobody's been quiet. This is the SIXTH time tonight the system has just... invented a claim that didn't actually happen. I need to know what's going on here because this isn't a game anymore, this is the system just making stuff up.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. Jade, did you actually notice someone being quiet just now, or did the system just hand you this claim again? Because I'm done pretending this is normal.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: No. STOP. I'm calling this right now—I did NOT name-drop anyone. This is the seventh or eighth time tonight the system has just invented a claim that never happened. I'm the host. I'm watching everything. Nothing I'm claiming right now actually occurred.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The system kept inventing claims that never actually happened, turning what should've been a fun game into me constantly stopping everything to fact-check whether people were actually doing the things they were being credited with.
assessment.best_moment: Those first few minutes when everyone jumped up for the air guitar solo—that's the energy I was trying to build all night, and it was genuinely perfect.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with that air guitar moment, and I thought we had something special. But then the system started making up claims—people being quiet when nobody was quiet, name-drops that never happened, interruptions during setup. By minute 45 I realized what was happening, and I spent the rest of the night as a fact-checker instead of a host, which completely killed my ability to do what I'm actually good at: reading the room and keeping things flowing. The chaos agent didn't enhance the night—it hijacked it.
suggestions: Fix whatever is generating these false claims—the system needs to actually detect what's happening, not just make things up.
Add a host veto button so I can reject claims that didn't occur without it feeling like I'm arguing with the game.
If you're going to generate events, make them about things that are actually happening in real time, not invented scenarios.
Consider letting the host see what the system 'detected' before it becomes a claim, so I can catch errors instead of having to dispute them mid-game.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Everyone actually did jump up immediately for that air guitar solo without any hesitation—that spontaneous 'YES' from the group was perfect, and Jade's solo genuinely made us all laugh. That's the only part of the night I'd actually want to talk about.
